strengths: Quantified impact on reporting and operations
weaknesses: Limited explicit DAX and semantic model examples
Data warehousing concept depth not emphasized
Unclear exposure to Power BI performance tuning
Few mentions of naming conventions/documentation standards
improvement_bullets: Developed robust Power BI semantic models by implementing DAX measures and calculated columns, resulting in improved data granularity and self-service reporting capabilities.
Performed extensive data validation and reconciliation between PostgreSQL source databases and Power BI datasets to ensure 100% accuracy in financial and operational reporting.
Applied data warehousing principles to structure relational datasets, optimizing report performance and ensuring high usability for cross-functional stakeholders.
Maintained rigorous documentation standards, including naming conventions and data lineage tracking, to ensure long-term sustainability and clarity of BI assets.
Collaborated with data engineering teams to refine ETL concepts and pipelines, ensuring consistent data flow from enterprise sources into analytics-ready schemas.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Business Intelligence Engineer opportunity with Ascot Group. I am interested in this opportunity because of Ascot’s reputation for excellence in the specialty risk underwriting space and its commitment to the 'Ascot Way' of collaborative, technology-driven problem solving. What interests me about working for Ascot Group is its 'Fusion Model'—the intersection of being client, risk, and technology-centric—which inspires me because it mirrors my own philosophy of using data to drive tangible business value. I believe my expertise in end-to-end data pipeline development and my proficiency in building actionable Power BI solutions would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on operational efficiency and data integrity. In fact, at Shine Masters, I replaced manual reporting with automated Power BI dashboards. By focusing on creating clean semantic models and DAX measures, I reduced data processing time by 40% and accelerated decision-making for executive leadership. Ultimately, I know that the Business Intelligence Engineer role at Ascot Group would allow me to bring my advanced SQL and modeling skills to the development and maintenance of critical reporting assets. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my data reconciliation skills. By implementing automated workflows and validating datasets against source systems, I ensured that team efficiency improved by 25% while maintaining high data accuracy. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in relational database management and Python-driven ETL processes in working on enterprise-level data integration at Ascot Group. I know that my technical proficiency in Power BI and SQL aligns with what it takes to succeed at the Business Intelligence Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
